seoTitle: How to Integrate ClickUp With Jira
metaDescription: Connect ClickUp with Jira so tasks and status updates flow between teams working across both platforms.
intro: When one part of an organization runs on ClickUp and another relies on Jira, keeping both in sync avoids duplicated work and missed updates. This guide covers connecting ClickUp and Jira together.

steps.heading: Choose your integration approach
steps.body: Decide between a dedicated connector app or an automation platform like Zapier or Make to bridge ClickUp and Jira.

steps.heading: Install and authorize the integration
steps.body: Set up the chosen tool and sign in to both ClickUp and Jira to grant the necessary permissions.

steps.heading: Map fields between the two tools
steps.body: Match up fields like task name, status, and assignee so data translates correctly between platforms.

steps.heading: Set the sync direction
steps.body: Decide whether updates should flow one way or sync both ways between ClickUp and Jira.

steps.heading: Run a test sync
steps.body: Create or update a task in one tool and confirm it reflects correctly in the other before relying on it for real work.

faq.question: Is there an official ClickUp and Jira integration?
faq.answer: There isn't a single built-in Atlassian integration; most teams use marketplace connectors or automation platforms instead.

faq.question: Will comments sync between ClickUp and Jira?
faq.answer: This depends on the specific integration tool, since not all connectors sync every field including comments.

faq.question: Can I sync only specific projects instead of everything?
faq.answer: Yes, most integration tools let you scope the sync to particular ClickUp spaces or Jira projects.

faq.question: Does this integration require a paid plan?
faq.answer: Many connector apps and automation platforms offer limited free tiers, but higher volume syncing usually requires a paid plan.
